publication.showAs: BOE núm. 87, de 9 de abril de 2026
publication.name: boe

TLCOrganization.showAs: Agencia Estatal Boletín Oficial del Estado

proprietary.akndiff:publisher: Agencia Estatal Boletín Oficial del Estado
proprietary.akndiff:researchNotes:
Friction log for ES / BOE-2026-087 (Phase 1 modeling pass):
1. JOURNAL ISSUE NUMBER FORMAT. Spanish BOE numbers issues with a plain integer (87, 88, 89...) reset annually. Chile's Diario Oficial uses a continuous integer (43.521, 43.522...). The schema's JournalTable.issueNumber is text, so both fit; but query "show me BOE núm. 87" needs to know whether to expect "87", "087", or "BOE-87". We use "87" (no leading zeros) to match BOE's own ELI URI pattern (.../boe/dias/2026/04/09/...). The padded form "087" appears in our nativeId (BOE-2026-087) so files sort lexicographically.
2. NATIVE ID OVERLAP. BOE assigns BOE-A-YYYY-NNNN identifiers to individual dispositions WITHIN an issue (e.g., Ley 1/2026 is BOE-A-2026-7967). The journal issue itself doesn't have an official BOE-X identifier — we synthesized "BOE-2026-087" for our nativeId. There's no risk of collision because BOE-A vs BOE- prefixes differ, but document the convention here.
3. MANY DISPOSITIONS PER ISSUE. A typical BOE issue contains dozens of dispositions (laws, royal decrees, orders, resolutions, announcements). Modeling each as a separate document with its own XML and componentRef from the journal issue is technically correct AKN but explodes the corpus. For Phase 1 we only model Ley 1/2026 (BOE-A-2026-7967). All other BOE-A-2026-7XXX dispositions of this same issue are ignored. A real production import would need to fetch the sumario API and componentRef every disposition; we'd pile up thousands of journal entries quickly.
4. NO componentRef LINKING. Per friction #3, we don't include <componentRef> entries pointing at every disposition of this issue (only one, the Ley). Means the journal page in the demo will look almost empty. Honest: we are missing data that exists.
5. SCOPE = "national" IS A FIXED VOCABULARY. The schema's scope field says 'national' | 'regional' | 'eu' | 'municipal'. Spain has BOE (national), but also DOG (Galicia), DOGC (Cataluña), BOA (Aragón), etc. — 17 autonomous community gazettes. "regional" works for those. But Spain also has the BORME (Boletín Oficial del Registro Mercantil), which is national but specialized. Plus IBERLEX historic compilations. The schema's enum is too coarse for Spain's real publication landscape. Documented; not a fix needed today.

longTitle.p: Boletín Oficial del Estado de la Jefatura del Estado, núm. 87, de 9 de abril de 2026.

publisher.p: Agencia Estatal Boletín Oficial del Estado

componentRef.showAs: Ley 1/2026, de 8 de abril, integral de impulso de la economía social (BOE-A-2026-7967, pp. 51121–51171)
